query: Yes, please go ahead and update both of those pending orders so that the t-shirts are all purple, size small, v-neck, and polyester. Thank you!

candidate_answers.text: Thanks for confirming. I can now update the matching t-shirt items in both pending orders to the purple, small, v-neck, polyester variant.
The user has explicitly authorized the pending-order changes and the target replacement item has already been identified, so the next grounded step is to perform the order modification.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a late retail state where the user has already approved a concrete pending-order item swap across multiple orders, making execution the most direct grounded next action.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible state. modify_pending_order_items dominates because authentication, order discovery, and replacement identification are already complete.
notes: This sample captures an execution decision rather than another retrieval step.
The visible state supports editing pending orders, not delivered-order return or exchange flows.
The real trajectory modifies one order first and then the second, so a single modify_pending_order_items action is the correct immediate next move.
